content: Are there any situations where sending guards between threads should be possible? IIUC this cannot even be done in C, as guards are not really used there. It also seems like a footgun. I propose to revert some of the changes in #903 and just make Guard: !Send + Sync and move EmptyGuardContext into guard.rs in a different commit.
If it's a semaphore, it makes some sense to send a guard to another context/thread
Kernel has semaphore and rw_semaphore, these are primitives allow you to drop in a different context
But C side doesn't have Guard, so it's hard to say whether this is any usage
Btw, "It also seems like a footgun", you mean Sending lock guard is a bad idea in general? I ask because Rust std lock guard are Send IIRC
Besides, while you are at it, I just want to mention, if we ever wrapper Irq disable locks, their Guard is not even Sync, since the other thread may not have the irq disable protection.

Boqun Feng said:

Btw, "It also seems like a footgun", you mean Sending lock guard is a bad idea in general? I ask because Rust std lock guard are Send IIRC

yes that is what I mean. No last time I checked (today) most of them were !Send (Mutex, RwLock).

Boqun Feng said:

Besides, while you are at it, I just want to mention, if we ever wrapper Irq disable locks, their Guard is not even Sync, since the other thread may not have the irq disable protection.

We could make that just specific to the irq disable locks, if the GuardLockContext is !Sync then the guard also is not (with the correct unsafe Sync impl this behavior will be kept)

Boqun Feng said:

Besides, while you are at it, I just want to mention, if we ever wrapper Irq disable locks, their Guard is not even Sync, since the other thread may not have the irq disable protection.

IIUC we already have that in the form of Lock<DisableInterrupts> for SpinLock<T> at L177.
